key_themes: Strong Q4 2025 earnings beat with revenue growth
Recent analyst PT raises (e.g. UBS to $400) but overall Hold consensus and modest 2026 outlook
Legacy product pressure (Prolia/Xgeva declines) and insider selling
summary: Amgen posted a solid Q4 2025 beat (EPS $5.29 vs $4.76 est., revenue +8.6% YoY) and delivered strong positive topline Phase 3 results for subcutaneous TEPEZZA on April 6 (77% proptosis response vs 19.6% placebo), prompting recent PT increases such as UBS lifting to $400.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AMGN/)[[2]](https://www.amgen.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/04/amgen-announces-positive-topline-phase-3-results-for-subcutaneous-tepezza-in-adults-living-with-moderate-to-severe-active-thyroid-eye-disease) Consensus remains Hold with average targets near the current ~$355 price, as many see 2026 as a transitional 'waiting year' with modest overall revenue growth and notable declines in mature products like Prolia and Xgeva.[[3]](https://public.com/stocks/amgn/forecast-price-target)[[4]](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/bernstein-downgrades-amgen-as-2026-lacks-nearterm-catalysts-4455836) The biggest risk is legacy revenue erosion, pipeline timing (e.g. MariTide), competitive threats, and continued insider/institutional selling pressure amid high debt levels.

recent_posts.highlight: Positive TEPEZZA Phase 3 topline (77% response rate, >3mm proptosis reduction) and UBS PT raise to $400 citing continued upside
$AMGN still a sleeping giant with bullish monthly hammer and technical setup targeting $360–370
Consensus Hold rating; some PT raises (Citigroup, Wells Fargo, Daiwa) but Bernstein earlier downgrade on limited 2026 catalysts and estimate cuts by Leerink
Multiple insider sales including EVP sales and large tax-related transactions totaling millions in shares
